name: God of Mummification | Egyptian Mythology
shortDescription: Anubis is the ancient Egyptian god of mummification, afterlife, and cemeteries, often depicted with the head of a jackal.
description: Anubis, known in ancient Egyptian as Inpu, is a prominent deity associated with death, mummification, embalming, the afterlife, cemeteries, tombs, and the Underworld. He is typically depicted as a man with the head of a jackal or in full jackal form, a representation likely stemming from the jackals that frequented cemeteries in ancient Egypt. His role was crucial in the process of mummification, where he oversaw the weighing of the heart ceremony in the Hall of Ma'at, determining whether the deceased was worthy to enter the afterlife. The heart was weighed against the feather of Ma'at (truth and justice). If the heart was lighter, the deceased could pass on to the realm of Osiris; if heavier, they would be devoured by Ammit. Anubis also guided souls through the Duat (the underworld). Although his parentage varies in different accounts, he is commonly believed to be the son of Nephthys and either Set or Osiris. With the rise of Osiris' cult, Anubis' role as ruler of the underworld gradually diminished, and he became more of a guide and protector of the dead, assisting Osiris in his judgment. Anubis was highly revered and played a central role in funerary rituals and beliefs throughout ancient Egyptian history.
epithets: He Who is Upon His Mountain

sources.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
